environment.auth: DEV MODE: Sign in as demo user (SEED_USER support@onlook.com)

assertions: iframe content includes 'Loading browser preview…' (literal SW HTML shell text from preview-sw.js htmlShell())
01_canvas_loading_browser_preview.proof: Frame URL routing in canvas/frame/view.tsx wrote the correct computed src for the ExpoBrowser branch. The service worker registered by preview-sw-register.tsx intercepted the request and served the HTML shell.

assertions: Publish dropdown contains: "Publishing isn't available in browser-preview mode yet."
Publish dropdown contains: "To publish this branch, switch its preview runtime back to CodeSandbox in branch settings. Your existing CSB sandbox is preserved while ExpoBrowser is active."
02_publish_disclaimer.proof: top-bar/publish/dropdown/index.tsx early-returned the ExpoBrowser disclaimer because activeBranch.sandbox.providerType === 'expo_browser'.

assertions: Settings modal opened via JD avatar → Settings menu
Modal sidebar shows Project / Site / Domain / Project / Versions / Mcp servers
Radio 'CodeSandbox (default — full Linux container)' present, unchecked
Radio 'Browser preview (free — Expo / React Native only)' present, CHECKED (matches branch.providerType='expo_browser')
user.getFeatureFlags tRPC procedure was called and returned { useExpoBrowserPreview: true }
04_settings_preview_runtime_radio.proof: settings-modal/project/index.tsx rendered the showPreviewRuntimeToggle conditional block. End-to-end: jsonb column → Drizzle query → tRPC procedure → React hook → conditional render.

assertions: DOM contains zero leaf elements with text content === 'Terminal'
Visible bottom-panel tabs are: 'Preview' and 'New Chat' only
05_terminal_tab_hidden.proof: SessionManager.createTerminalSessions read provider.getCapabilities().supportsTerminal (false for ExpoBrowserProvider) and skipped creating the interactive terminal CLISessionImpl. Only the task session was created.

assertions.actual: iframe contains [preview error] SyntaxError + Bare import error, NOT App.tsx text
assertions.expected: iframe DOM innerText contains fixture App.tsx text

assertions.actual: Two preview errors: SyntaxError on preload script, Bare import URL hit IIFE require

06.title: Real bundle renders App.tsx output in canvas iframe
06.notes: Pipeline executes correctly through bundle production (4 modules, entry index.ts). Two architecture bugs prevent the IIFE from running in the iframe. Required Storage RLS workaround (verify_anon_select policy) to unblock the Vfs population — TR1.3 RLS migration is correct shape but the browser-side Supabase client does not pass authenticated JWT to Storage requests. See FOUND-R1.7 below.

07.title: Edit a file in the editor, iframe reflects within 2s
blockers: scenario 06 must pass first; FOUND-06a and FOUND-06b block the entire R3 path
07.notes: Cannot meaningfully verify hot-reload until scenario 06 (initial render) actually shows DOM content from the fixture.

not_yet_verified_in_this_run.name: 06_chat_agent_provider_no_shell
not_yet_verified_in_this_run.reason: Requires a real LLM round-trip via the chat panel — slow, model-dependent, and the agent has to choose to call bash_read on its own. Will be added as a follow-up scenario when a deterministic chat fixture is in place.
not_yet_verified_in_this_run.alternative_evidence: BashReadTool.handle and BashEditTool.handle have unit tests that mock the provider with supportsShell=false; the per-tool capability check returns PROVIDER_NO_SHELL deterministically. The tRPC + capability flow is exercised by the integration test in packages/code-provider/src/providers/expo-browser/__tests__/integration.test.ts (5/5 pass).

not_yet_verified_in_this_run.name: 07_npm_install_via_interceptor
not_yet_verified_in_this_run.reason: Same — requires the chat agent to actually call terminal_command('npm install ...').
not_yet_verified_in_this_run.alternative_evidence: The runCommand interceptor test in expo-browser/__tests__/integration.test.ts:runCommand routes through interceptor verifies the full path (writeFile package.json → run interceptor → re-read patched file) against real local Supabase Storage.

not_yet_verified_in_this_run.name: 08_isomorphic_git_status
not_yet_verified_in_this_run.reason: GitManager.init capability gate short-circuits cleanly for ExpoBrowser branches in v1 (real isomorphic-git swap is Sprint 4 stretch). Verifying it does the right thing requires opening the commits panel and seeing it stay empty, which is an absence-of-error verification rather than a positive assertion.

issues_found_during_verification.description: Pre-existing schema drift: conversations table was missing an agent_type column that the TS code expects. Project route 500'd until I added the column with `ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN agent_type agent_type`. Not caused by Wave 0–J — pre-existing tech debt that surfaced because no one had run the project route end-to-end recently.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Manual SQL: CREATE TYPE agent_type AS ENUM ('default', 'mcp'); ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN agent_type agent_type; — should be made into a proper migration in a follow-up commit

issues_found_during_verification.description: Pre-existing inferPageFromUrl util in packages/utility/src/urls.ts:74 calls `new URL(url)` on the persisted frame.url. If the URL is relative (e.g. '/preview/...'), this throws 'Failed to construct URL: Invalid URL'. The util needs to accept relative URLs OR the SW preview URLs need to be absolute.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Worked around by setting frame.url to an absolute http://127.0.0.1:3001/preview/... in the verification test data. Should be fixed in inferPageFromUrl to gracefully handle relative URLs (the service worker preview URLs are relative by design).

issues_found_during_verification.description: next dev --turbo crashed with V8 OOM during the first verification run. SWC native binding used a lot of heap during incremental compilation. Plain `next dev` (no --turbo) worked fine.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Document in the verification README that turbo mode can OOM during long verification sessions. Consider adding NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=8192' to the dev script.

issues_found_during_verification.description: On the first attempt with the dev server, user.getFeatureFlags was never called even though the settings modal Project tab rendered. Suspected cause: hot-reload didn't pick up the new tRPC procedure that was added on the integration branch. Restart the dev server (no turbo) and the procedure fires correctly. There may be a Next.js dev server state-cache issue with newly-added tRPC procedures, but I couldn't reproduce after the restart, so leaving it as a watch-out rather than a fix.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: If anyone hits 'Preview runtime radio not appearing in settings', restart the dev server fresh.

issues_found_during_verification.description: The /project/[id] route was redirecting to /see-a-demo because checkUserSubscriptionAccess returned false for the demo SEED_USER. The verification pre-seeded a row in legacy_subscriptions for support@onlook.com to bypass.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Pre-seed legacy_subscriptions in the verification harness setup script. The fix in production is to either grant SEED_USER an active subscription on local seed, or skip the subscription check in DEV MODE login.

issues_found_during_verification.title: Preload script loaded as classic <script> but is ESM, throws SyntaxError on iframe load
issues_found_during_verification.evidence: iframe console: [preview] Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token export from /onlook-preload-script.js. The shell injects <script src=/onlook-preload-script.js> as classic; the file ships ESM with top-level export.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Either (a) change to <script type=module src=/onlook-preload-script.js> in htmlShell, OR (b) drop the preload script entirely from the ExpoBrowser preview shell — it is for click-to-edit penpal and not strictly needed for fixture rendering.

issues_found_during_verification.title: Bundle architecture mismatch — IIFE require shim cannot resolve esm.sh URL specs from bare-import-rewriter
issues_found_during_verification.evidence: iframe console: Error: Bare import https://esm.sh/react-native-web?bundle&external=... reached IIFE require; the rewriter should have replaced it. Stack: __resolve at bundle.js:104. The rewriter rewrote bare specs to esm.sh URLs in the source. Sucrase imports transform converted them to require() calls. The IIFE require shim throws on any spec not starting with . or /.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Architectural change required. Cleanest path: switch the bundle output from CJS-style IIFE to ES module syntax (emit import * as X from URL ...), serve as <script type=module>, and rely on the iframe importmap to resolve the bare names. The current IIFE wrapper (TR2.4) cannot dynamically fetch URL modules synchronously — browsers do not support sync import. An interim hack would be to pre-fetch all URL deps at bundle time and inline them, but that defeats the CDN advantage and recreates the original Metro complexity inside the host package.

issues_found_during_verification.title: Browser-side Supabase client does not pass authenticated JWT to Storage requests
issues_found_during_verification.evidence: After DEV MODE login, the Onlook auth cookie is set (sb-127-auth-token) and tRPC user.get returns the SEED_USER. But the editor.fileSystem.listFiles() returns 0 files even though Storage SELECT RLS allows the project owner. Direct psql with SET LOCAL request.jwt.claim.sub = SEED_USER works. The browser-side @supabase/supabase-js client used by ExpoBrowserProvider Storage operations is a different GoTrueClient instance than the one Onlook uses for auth (the Multiple GoTrueClient instances detected warning is the symptom).
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Audit how ExpoBrowserProvider acquires its Supabase client. Either inject the existing authenticated client from the editor session, or call supabase.auth.setSession() with the JWT from the Onlook cookie before any Storage call. Workaround for verification only: a permissive verify_anon_select policy on storage.objects that allows any authenticated/anon user to SELECT from expo-projects bucket. This was applied manually to local DB and is NOT in any migration file.

issues_found_during_verification.title: TR1.5 firstPullComplete races with attachBrowserMetro when sync engine is reused
issues_found_during_verification.evidence: Console shows attachBrowserMetro called twice in quick succession (msgid 6775, 6811) for the same branch. Both initial bundles fail because both run before sync.firstPullComplete actually populates the Vfs. The Option A fix in TR1.5 awaits firstPullComplete on a SHARED CodeProviderSync instance — when refCount > 1, the second consumer gets the already-resolved promise even though the second sync.start was a no-op.
issues_found_during_verification.fix: Make firstPullComplete a per-call promise rather than per-instance. Or gate attachBrowserMetro on this.fs.listAll().length > 0 as a defensive belt-and-suspenders check (Option B from TR1.5).

summary.notes: Scenarios 01-05 still passing from parent queue. Scenario 06 now has actual evidence: pipeline through bundle production works (R0+R1+R2 verified end-to-end), but two architectural bugs (FOUND-06a + FOUND-06b) prevent the iframe runtime from rendering. Scenario 07 blocked on 06. Scenarios 08-13 require Phase H Container running locally (TH1.1 not landed) + Phase Q UI walk. Scenario 14 is human-only (real phone scan).
